name: loguru
summary: Python logging that doesn't make you want to cry — structured, colorful, zero-config, with automatic exception tracebacks.

story.problem: Python's built-in logging module requires boilerplate setup (handlers, formatters, loggers) just to print a colored timestamp. It's powerful but the API is from 2003 and shows its age.
story.mental_model: Loguru has a single pre-configured logger object. You import it and call logger.info(). Under the hood it handles formatting, colors, log rotation, JSON output, and exception capture. Add sinks (files, services) with logger.add().
story.when_to_use: Any Python application that needs logging — scripts, APIs, workers. Especially good for: colorized console output during development, structured JSON logs for production, automatic exception tracing.
story.when_not_to_use: If your team's infrastructure is already built around stdlib logging and you can't change it easily — loguru can interop but it adds complexity.

alternatives.name: structlog
alternatives.reason: Structured logging with stdlib compatibility, better for large teams

alternatives.name: logging
alternatives.reason: stdlib, no dependency, required by some frameworks

nodes.label: logger
nodes.summary: Pre-configured singleton — just import and use

nodes.label: logger.add()
nodes.summary: Add a sink (file, stderr, function, service)

nodes.label: logger.catch()
nodes.summary: Decorator/context manager that captures exceptions with full traceback

nodes.label: logger.bind()
nodes.summary: Add structured context to all subsequent log calls

nodes.label: serialize=True
nodes.summary: Output logs as JSON — great for production log aggregators

nodes.label: Log rotation
nodes.summary: Auto-rotate log files by size, time, or count

tasks.title: Replace print statements with proper logging

steps.label: Zero-config usage
steps.code:
from loguru import logger

logger.debug('Debugging value: {}', some_var)
logger.info('Server started on port {}', 8000)
logger.warning('Cache miss — fetching from DB')
logger.error('Failed to connect: {}', exc)
logger.success('All 42 records processed')

steps.label: Add file sink with rotation
steps.code:
logger.add(
    'logs/app.log',
    level='INFO',
    rotation='10 MB',   # new file when this size is hit
    retention='7 days', # delete old files
    compression='zip',  # compress rotated files
)

steps.label: JSON sink for log aggregators
steps.code:
import sys
from loguru import logger

logger.remove()  # remove default stderr handler
logger.add(sys.stderr, serialize=True, level='INFO')  # JSON output
logger.add('logs/app.json', serialize=True, rotation='1 day')

# Bind request context
req_logger = logger.bind(request_id='abc-123', user_id=42)
req_logger.info('Processing payment', amount=99.99)
